title: lychnis quadridentata
intro: Lychnis quadridentata, also known as four-toothed lychnis or wolf's-claw, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its roots and leaves. The health benefits of Lychnis quadridentata include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions or decoctions of its roots and leaves, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
